question_text: During a thunderstorm, lightning forms when charge builds up between clouds and the ground. Explain why the lightning is so powerful and dangerous.
correct_answer: Enormous amounts of charge build up between the cloud and ground / The electrical potential difference is extremely large / This causes a massive electrical discharge through the air
explanation: Lightning occurs when static charge accumulates to an enormous level between clouds and the Earth. When this charge is released as a discharge, it travels through the air at extremely high speed and temperature, making it very dangerous.
